variables.name: Compute access regime (chips + CUDA + cloud infrastructure)

actor_readings.reading: Most binding constraint — blocked from H100-class chips and EUV; Ascend/SMIC catching up but a generation behind. High confidence (#25,#A32,#A6).
Chip access gated by US bilateral deals (Stargate-analog); capital and energy abundant but compute is permission-based. Medium confidence (#34,#110).
Constrained less by chips than by data-center energy/grid and permitting (Ireland/NL moratoria), a physical ceiling money cannot buy through unilaterally. Medium confidence (#129).
Holds the chokepoint; frontier compute is not the binding limit. High confidence (#25,#A31).

variables.current_reading: US/allied-controlled at the leading edge and bifurcating. The NVIDIA GPU + CUDA middleware + TSMC/ASML fab stack is US-leverageable end-to-end (#25,#126,#A31,#A32); BIS controls keep China ~7nm-equivalent (#A32). High confidence this is the most operationally direct protection layer (#25).
variables.trajectory: Single-node chokepoints are among the most durable rents but reliably accelerate adversary indigenization; catch-up window 5-7yr (#A32,#A6). Efficiency gains (V5) can partially route around within that window. Clock: 2-5yr. Analogy breaks because model weights/software are copyable at near-zero cost unlike fabs (#A22).
variables.base_rate: ASML/EUV (#A32) and ITAR/Wassenaar/BIS (#A6) show input-layer chokepoints impose multi-year delays and function as geopolitical weapons; Wintel/CUDA (#A31) shows rent accretes to the irreplaceable interface layer. Breaks: software workarounds (distillation, sparsity) historically route around hardware chokepoints faster than fabs can be replicated.

variables.name: Training-data legality regime

actor_readings.reading: Art.4 TDM opt-out is a distinct ex-ante exclusion that protects home publishers/media groups and blocks future EU scraping regardless of copyright subsistence (#113,#A8). Medium-high confidence.

Fair-use lineage (Betamax→Google Books) plus CFAA/ToS and DMCA §1201 sub-layers govern access; likely resolves to a licensing market that grandfathers first-movers (#106,#112,#120,#A3,#A13). Medium confidence.

variables.current_reading: Contested and litigated. US trending toward fair-use + emerging bilateral licensing deals; EU already has an ex-ante opt-out property right (#113). Whichever way it resolves, incumbents who trained pre-2023/pre-Aug-2025 are grandfathered (#4,#43) — the temporal ratchet is the load-bearing fact, not the eventual doctrine. Medium confidence (#8).
variables.trajectory: Backward-looking ratchet: enforcement is prospective, so delay enlarges the moat (#4). Licensing markets historically do emerge (ASCAP #A33, SoundExchange #A34, Hatch-Waxman #A2) but grandfather incumbents. Synthetic/RLHF/AI-output-provenance routes (#13,#26,#40,#119) may moot the pretraining-corpus question entirely. Clock: 2-4yr litigation.
variables.base_rate: Betamax #A3, Google Books #A13, news bargaining #A12, ASCAP/BMI #A33, SoundExchange #A34, Hatch-Waxman #A2 all converge: the technology survives, a blanket-license regime emerges, first-movers keep permanently cheaper model lineages. Breaks: web-scale corpus can't be un-published (unlike discrete clinical/song catalogs) and authorship is diffuse, making 'exclusivity' economically odd.

variables.name: EU regulatory trajectory (AI Act durability & enforcement)
state_space: soft-law hollowing via Code of Practice

actor_readings.reading: Faces a self-deregulation dilemma: rolling back removes its own drag but also dissolves the moat thesis; four intervention points (rule text/CoP/enforcement capacity/member-state lobbying) each produce different winners (#93,#99,#100). Medium confidence.
Hyperscalers' rational position is selective enforcement (rules on EU players, not themselves), not repeal — consistent with observed CoP shaping (#97). Medium-high confidence.

variables.current_reading: Active rollback/softening vector, not a stable constraint. Draghi report + 2025 digital-omnibus + successive CoP softening on copyright/eval/incident thresholds (#94,#95); GDPR under-enforcement base rate suggests rules survive on paper while enforcement lags years (#28,#96). Medium confidence the Act does NOT bite as written.
variables.trajectory: Softening; extraterritorial enforceability against non-EU-hosted models is doubtful (#15,#27). Clock: 2025-2027 live legislative process (#95). Downside case (GMO #A28) is unrecoverable domestic-industry loss; upside case (REACH #A27) is a working compliance moat contingent on ~25%+ market share driving a Brussels Effect.
variables.base_rate: OSI #A5 (govt frameworks arrive after the network-effects winner is deployed and lose), REACH #A27 (fixed compliance cost entrenches incumbents IF share is high enough), GMO #A28 (precaution destroys domestic capability while consumption continues via imports), cultural exception #A15 (rules survive trade challenge but don't close the capability gap). Breaks: AI compounds via data flywheel, making early disadvantage geometrically worse than in static-good precedents.

variables.name: Compliance-as-product / infrastructure bundling moat

actor_readings.reading: Hyperscalers own this layer and convert regulation from cost into revenue + lock-in; cloud-exclusivity clauses foreclose vertically (#59,#60,#71,#61). High confidence.
Sovereign-cloud/procurement rules (#41) partly wall a domestic segment but do not produce a competing bundle at scale. Medium confidence.

variables.current_reading: Currently hyperscaler-bundled and sticky: Azure/Bedrock/Vertex ship compliance posture as a feature (#59,#60), FedRAMP High is a de facto federal duopoly (#61), IP indemnification adds procurement stickiness only large legal reserves can offer (#107). Independent compliance SaaS (Credo/Holistic) is nascent (#64,#66). Medium-high confidence.
variables.trajectory: Consolidation is the default (cloud-security tooling acquisition history #66); antitrust attention could interrupt but is timeline-mismatched (V8). Critically, requires an EU/regulated rule to exist (#97) — so it co-moves with V3. Clock: slow, contract-cycle sticky.
variables.base_rate: Basel III #A26, REACH #A27, SOX #A29: fixed per-entity compliance cost structurally entrenches scale and legitimizes the largest players. Breaks: third-party attestation accepted as regulatory-equivalent would commoditize the bundle (#64,#68).

variables.name: Open-weight + efficiency trajectory
state_space: open commoditizes the model layer, value migrates one layer up

actor_readings.reading: Open weights are the primary escape from compute/data moats and export control (DeepSeek; RISC-V-style neutral-stack logic) (#6,#A22). Medium-high confidence.
Open-weight release is a regulatory-arbitrage lever (AI Act OSS-GPAI exemption) and Mistral's model, not altruism (#109). Medium confidence.
Meta/Llama commoditize rivals' model layer deliberately to protect adjacent (ads/cloud) revenue; value re-accretes to hyperscaler inference (#A19,#A20). Medium-high confidence.

variables.current_reading: Open weights trail the closed frontier by roughly one generation but track closely (DeepSeek R1 near-zero cost #6); efficiency is actively pressuring the EU 10^25-FLOP systemic-risk threshold (#130,#9). Model-collapse (#40) vs synthetic-data-works (#13) is unresolved, but RLHF/preference data (#26) and national-language corpora (#101) remain non-commoditized. Medium confidence.
variables.trajectory: History is emphatic: openness breaks the layer-N moat but value re-accretes to layer N+1 (managed inference/infra) — Linux #A19, Android #A20, Kubernetes #A21, Firefox→Chrome #A23. RISC-V #A22 shows neutral-jurisdiction escape can't beat the chokepoint one layer up (inference compute). Clock: FAST — 12-18mo generations, the quickest-moving variable in the graph.
variables.base_rate: #A19-A23: open-source reliably commoditizes a proprietary standards moat, and the funder/distributor (not the builder) captures the value. Breaks: AI carries copyright/geopolitical attack surface browsers/OSes lacked (#A23), and permissive licenses allow proprietary forks that weaken the commons (#A19).

variables.name: Protocol / interface standard control

actor_readings.reading: Anthropic (MCP), OpenAI (API shape), Google (A2A) are positioned to seed the mandated interface; MCP-as-open-standard-proprietary-ecosystem is the Android play (#3,#11). Medium confidence.
5G playbook (state-backed standards-body capture) is available for AI protocols moving through IEEE/ISO (#44). Low-medium confidence.

variables.current_reading: Early and unsettled. MCP / function-calling schemas are emerging as de facto integration standards (#3,#11); the open-protocol historical record is genuinely mixed — TCP/IP resisted capture (#14) but 5G/ITU was captured by a state-backed actor (#44). Low-medium confidence on direction.
variables.trajectory: Interface capture works but is legally contestable (Oracle v. Google favored interoperability #A24); SEP-into-standard is the durable rent path (Qualcomm #A36, Rambus #A16). If EU AI Act / CEN-CENELEC mandates a standard, FRAND obligations attach and a transatlantic licensing arbitrage opens. Clock: 3-7yr.
variables.base_rate: Qualcomm #A36 and Rambus #A16 show standards-embedded IP yields 60-70% margins and survives antitrust; IBM PCM #A9 and SWIFT #A11 show interface control is durable but eventually cracked by a new layer or commoditization; TCP/IP #A5/#14 is the counter. Breaks: post-Alice software/protocol patents are harder to enforce, and weightless distribution makes 'no license, no chips' hard to replicate.

variables.name: Talent geography / mobility

actor_readings.reading: Concentrated via §16600 non-compete asymmetry (#49), visa-tied leverage (#48), trade-secret/DTSA barriers (#50); but a leaky moat — returnees transfer knowledge to China (#53,#57). Medium-high confidence.

BINDING constraint. Blue Card/Researcher Directive structurally failed (#52); equity-comp/tax gap likely unaddressable by regulation alone (#58); founders leave for US (Mistral ex-DeepMind) (#51). High confidence this dominates the capability gap over regulation.
Explicit state pipeline capture (CSC scholarships) + return programs make talent a contested resource it is partially winning back (#53,#55). Medium confidence.

variables.current_reading: US-concentrated but leaky. Bay Area cluster anchored by California's non-compete near-ban (#49) plus H-1B/green-card lock-in leverage (#48); but returnee flows to China are material (DeepSeek/Baidu teams #53) and EU suffers persistent outflow (#51,#52). Medium-high confidence.
variables.trajectory: Sticky and slow (generational). EU deficit is structural — equity/tax wedge, 27 fragmented labor markets (#58,#52). US leaks via returnees; remote work partially decouples geography from visas (#54). Clock: slowest-moving variable alongside V9.
variables.base_rate: Minitel #A14 (a state-backed closed system delays adoption of the superior open standard and accelerates brain drain — the sharpest EU warning); Airbus #A25 is the counter-case (patient, CONSOLIDATED public capital can overcome US incumbency, but EU AI funding is fragmented across Mistral/Aleph Alpha/etc., resembling pre-1970 European aviation). Breaks: remote/contractor work weakens visa-as-chokepoint (#54).

variables.name: Antitrust / structural intervention capacity

actor_readings.reading: Structural-remedy bias + Art.22 referral catch-net + retrospective market studies (CMA) — the most threatening avenue but slow (#72,#75,#76). Medium confidence.
Behavioral-remedy regime (decays as APIs change), HSR gap on acqui-hires (#69,#75), but CFIUS one-way inbound wall channels exits to domestic hyperscalers (#103). Medium confidence.

variables.current_reading: Mostly ineffective against AI velocity: CMA/FTC/DG-Comp investigations run 12-24mo, remedies 3-7yr, vs 12-18mo model generations (#79). EU/UK gained below-threshold call-in (Art.22, DMCC #75) while the US HSR framework leaves acqui-hires/sub-$119M deals unreviewable; 'foundation model market' definition unresolved (#77). Medium confidence.
variables.trajectory: Timeline mismatch is structural (#79); aggressive scrutiny can perversely concentrate by foreclosing the VC exit path (#74,#78). Multi-jurisdiction divergence lets incumbents forum-shop (#80). Clock: 3-7yr — too slow to bind.
variables.base_rate: Standard Oil #A35 (infrastructure-layer rent survives even a 34-way breakup), Computer Inquiries/Carterfone #A17 (how the infra/service line is drawn compounds for decades), EU energy unbundling #A18 and IBM #A9 (partial success over 13-18yr, incumbents survive). Breaks: AI generations are an order of magnitude faster than any enforcement cycle.

variables.name: Fragmentation regime structure (bloc count)

actor_readings.reading: Full walled-stack endpoint is viable because domestic market is large enough to sustain an independent ecosystem (#16,#A10). Medium-high confidence.
Lacks China's scale and decoupling willingness — fragmentation likely yields compliance-overhead asymmetry, not a genuine alternative ecosystem (#A10,#A5). Medium confidence.

variables.current_reading: Trending multipolar. US/China/EU tripartite plus India as a distinct fourth regime (#31), Japan light-touch fifth pole (#108), and UAE/Singapore/UK arbitrage havens (#17). Convergence vectors exist (CoE Convention #32, ISO 42001 #68, adequacy tracks #65) but are advisory/uneven. Medium confidence.
variables.trajectory: Deliberate fragmentation provides medium-term (≈decade) protection but is dissolved by a new tech layer OR sustained only by a large domestic market (#A1,#A4,#A7,#A10). Convergence toward a common floor is possible only after a catastrophic incident — and even then jurisdictions likely diverge (#38 vs #39,#46). Clock: decade+.
variables.base_rate: GSM/CDMA #A1, rail gauge #A4, NTSC/PAL/SECAM #A7, Great Firewall #A10, SWIFT #A11: fragmentation redistributes hardware/infrastructure rent and persists where switching costs and market scale are high, but rarely blocks the application-layer winner. Breaks: software forking cost is near-zero, lowering fragmentation pressure vs physical-standard precedents.

variables.name: Vertical / sectoral regulated-entity value capture
state_space: horizontal foundation models win across verticals

actor_readings.reading: Regulated-entity structural position (FDA clearance holder, SR 11-7 owner) captures value above the horizontal AI layer; incumbents cannot be substituted away because they hold the regulator relationship and liability (#81,#82,#84). Medium confidence.

variables.current_reading: Strong per-vertical moats today: FDA 510(k) predicate lock-in and HIPAA/clinical-dataset control in medical (#82,#87), SR 11-7 in banking (#84), DO-178C exclusion of ML in aviation (#86); but RWE/De Novo/synthetic pathways are already eroding the medical moat (#124,#90), and EHR vendors intermediate the hospital data moat (#88). Medium confidence.
variables.trajectory: Depends whether foundation-model general capability outpaces domain-specific regulated-data advantage (#91 crux). Vertical certification cycles move slowly (FAA 5-10yr) and predate/ignore the AI Act (#85). Clock: 3-5yr; erosion via RWE is live (#124).
variables.base_rate: Hatch-Waxman #A2 (clinical-trial-data exclusivity as the direct analog — data, not formula, is protected; grandfathers originators). Aviation certification durably excludes new methods. Breaks: FDA De Novo pathway needs no predicate and RWE authorizations already issued (#124), so the vertical moat is empirically softening.

edges.note: Leading-edge compute is a prerequisite to train even efficient/open models at frontier scale; export controls gate China's open-weight ambitions (#25,#A32).
Efficiency gains + open pre-trained weights relieve compute scarcity — DeepSeek-class models route around the GPU chokepoint (#6,#130,#A22).
Hyperscalers control both GPU allocation and the compliance bundle; cloud-exclusivity clauses convert compute control into vertical foreclosure of the model layer (#71,#59,#60).
Compute bifurcation (BIS controls + weight-export framework) is the upstream driver of bloc fragmentation (#25,#125,#A6).
Same data-exclusivity logic — protect the expensive curated input, not the model — operates in copyright (V2) and in HIPAA/FDA regulated-data moats (#87,#4,#A2).
Open weights trained on pre-enforcement data, plus synthetic/RLHF routes, bypass the training-data licensing moat entirely (#6,#109,#13).
A commoditized open base model weakens protocol/interface capture because the high-value layer is no longer scarce (#6,#14).
Efficiency erodes the EU 10^25-FLOP systemic-risk threshold faster than the Act adapts, potentially reversing 'GPAI rules entrench incumbents' (#130,#9).
Compliance-as-product requires the rule to exist — if the EU rolls back (V3), the hyperscaler compliance moat (V4) loses its premise (#97,#100).
EU self-deregulation would collapse the tripartite fragmentation premise, pulling toward US/Japan voluntary-norm convergence (#93,#95,#108).
The EU cannot build a competitive frontier/open model without reversing its talent deficit; talent gates capability more than regulation does (#51,#56,#A14).
US non-compete asymmetry + visa lock-in concentrate the researchers needed to push the frontier, though the container leaks via returnees (#49,#48,#53).
Antitrust access/structural remedies are the only tool that could pry open the compute+compliance bundle — but the enforcement clock is too slow to bind (#73,#79,#A35).
Structural remedies could in principle break hyperscaler compute self-preferencing, but Standard Oil shows infra rent survives breakup (#A35,#A18).
Protocol capture and compliance bundling are the same infrastructure-layer enclosure move; SEP/FRAND rents and certified-envelope lock-in reinforce each other (#11,#A36,#60).
If model collapse is a real ceiling (#40), incumbents' pre-collapse high-quality corpora become permanent — strengthening V2's ratchet and weakening V5's substitution.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will China achieve volume production of a domestically-fabbed AI training accelerator at a 7nm-or-better node by 31 Dec 2027?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: Public disclosure (company earnings, credible teardown e.g. TechInsights, or government statement) that a China-fabbed AI accelerator at ≤7nm is shipping in >10,000-unit volume by 2027-12-31.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will a US court ruling or major settlement establish a compulsory or collective training-data licensing mechanism by 31 Dec 2027?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: A US federal ruling or a settlement in the NYT/authors/Getty class of cases that creates an ongoing blanket/collective license or rate-setting body for AI training data, reported by a major legal outlet, by 2027-12-31.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will the EU formally adopt legislation or an implementing act that materially reduces AI Act GPAI provider obligations by 31 Dec 2026?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: An EU digital-omnibus or amending act entering into force by 2026-12-31 that delays or lightens GPAI transparency/evaluation/copyright obligations relative to the 2024 text, per the Official Journal.

forecast_signals.binary_question: As of 31 Dec 2026, will at least one open-weight model rank in the top 5 of a major public capability leaderboard (e.g. LMArena)?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: A freely downloadable open-weight model occupies a top-5 position on a widely-cited public leaderboard (LMArena or equivalent) on 2026-12-31.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will a US-origin AI interface protocol (MCP, function-calling schema, or A2A) be incorporated by reference into an EU-harmonized or ISO/IEEE AI standard by 31 Dec 2027?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: A CEN-CENELEC/ISO/IEEE published standard or EU harmonized-standard citation referencing a US-origin agent/tool-use protocol by name, by 2027-12-31.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will net migration of top-tier AI researchers between the EU and the US remain in US favor (EU net outflow) over calendar 2026?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: A credible tracker (e.g. MacroPolo/Interconnected, Stanford AI Index, or LinkedIn migration data) reporting net EU→US flow of top-tier AI researchers exceeding net US→EU flow for 2026.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will the CMA, FTC, or DG COMP impose a binding structural or mandated-access remedy on a hyperscaler–AI-lab investment by 31 Dec 2027?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: A legally binding remedy (divestiture, mandated API/data access, or equivalent) targeting Microsoft-OpenAI, Amazon/Google-Anthropic, or similar, published by the authority by 2027-12-31 (voluntary commitments excluded).

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will an EU or member-state authority accept a non-hyperscaler third-party attestation (e.g. ISO 42001 or a compliance-SaaS report) as presumptive AI Act conformity evidence by 31 Dec 2027?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: Official EU/NMSA guidance or a harmonized-standard designation recognizing a model-agnostic third-party attestation as presumption-of-conformity for AI Act obligations, by 2027-12-31.

forecast_signals.binary_question: By 31 Dec 2026, will a model trained below the 10^25-FLOP threshold match top-tier frontier performance on a major public benchmark?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: A model with credibly reported/estimated training compute below 10^25 FLOP scores within 3 points of the leading model on a recognized benchmark (e.g. MMLU-Pro, GPQA, or LMArena Elo top tier) by 2026-12-31.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will the US impose enforceable export controls on frontier AI model weights (reviving/replacing the Diffusion Framework) by 31 Dec 2027?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: A BIS rule in force by 2027-12-31 that controls export of frontier model weights above a capability/compute threshold to specified countries, per the Federal Register.

binding_constraint_by_actor.reading: Binding stave = V5 (open-weight + efficiency). The US is not compute-, talent-, or capital-constrained; the one variable that erodes its frontier/market-protection position is commoditization of the model layer by open weights and efficiency gains (DeepSeek), against which history says value merely re-accretes to the compute/compliance infrastructure the US still controls (#6,#130,#A19-A23). Secondary: self-inflicted regulatory volatility (#45). Medium confidence.

Binding stave = V1 (compute access). Chips + EUV + CUDA are the chokepoint the US directly controls; everything else (talent-return #53, open weights #6, domestic market scale #A10) China can supply. Clears only on a 5-7yr indigenization clock (#25,#A32,#A6). High confidence.

Binding stave = V7 (talent) — NOT its own regulation (V3). The causal chain runs through the equity-comp/tax structural deficit and researcher outflow (#51,#52,#58); sovereign compute (V1 energy, #129) is a secondary compounding constraint; the AI Act is something the EU is actively hollowing, so it cannot be the binding moat/drag. Minitel #A14 is the warning, Airbus #A25 the only escape (consolidated patient capital), which the fragmented EU startup field lacks. Medium-high confidence.

Binding stave = V7 + V1 jointly. Capital (SWF equity capture #110) and energy/data-center capacity (#34) are abundant; what is missing is indigenous talent and US-permissioned chip access. Its strategy is financial capture and haven-positioning (#17,#110), not domestic capability. Medium confidence.

Binding stave = V7 + capital/compute. India protects domestic data without blocking foreign models and leads with procurement/subsidy rather than regulation (#31); its constraint is building frontier capability and compute, not a regulatory moat. Language-corpus advantage (#101) is a genuine but narrow home-field asset. Medium confidence.

summary: The graph shows a topic with no single "winner" but a clear division of labor among binding constraints operating on very different clocks. Compute (V1) is simultaneously China's binding stave and a US-controlled mutual chokepoint that clears only on a 5-7yr indigenization clock (#25,#A32,#A6). Training-data legality (V2) is not decisive for *who* leads — it is a backward-looking ratchet that grandfathers whoever trained first regardless of how litigation resolves (#4,#43,#A2,#A13). The single most important cross-cutting variable is open-weight + efficiency (V5): it substitutes for compute scarcity, bypasses the data-legality moat, undercuts the EU's own FLOP threshold, and commoditizes protocol capture — historically breaking the layer-N moat while value re-accretes one layer up to infrastructure/compliance bundling (V4) that hyperscalers control (#A19-A23,#59-61). The structurally surprising read is on the EU: its binding constraint is talent (V7), not its own regulation (V3) — which it is actively hollowing (#93-100) — so the whole "regulatory moat vs regulatory drag" frame is contingent on a rule the EU may not keep. Net: the US frontier moat erodes on a fast open/efficiency clock; China's compute constraint clears on a slow indigenization clock; the EU's talent constraint barely clears at all; value tends to settle at the compute + compliance-bundling infrastructure layer regardless of who wins the model layer.
